name: Nextion TFT LCD Display
description: The `nextion` display platform allows you to use Nextion LCD displays ([datasheet](https://nextion.itead.cc/resources/datasheets/), [iTead](https://www.itead.cc/display/nextion.html)) with ESPHome.

config_entries.label: UART ID
config_entries.description: The ID of the [UART Bus](https://esphome.io/components/uart) you wish to use for this display. Specify this when you have multiple UART configurations.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to call the lambda to update the display. Defaults to `5s`.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Auto Wake On Touch
config_entries.description: If set to `true`, the Nextion will be configured to wake from sleep when touched.

config_entries.label: Brightness
config_entries.description: When specified, the display brightness will be set to this value at boot.

config_entries.label: Command Spacing
config_entries.description: Sets the minimum time between commands sent to the Nextion display. A higher value can help prevent buffer overflows but will result in slower interface updates. Range is `0-255ms`. Defaults to `0ms` (disabled).

config_entries.label: Dump Device Info
config_entries.description: Shows device information (model, firmware version, serial number, flash size) in the configuration dump. When disabled, device info is only logged during connection establishment to save memory. Defaults to false. .

config_entries.label: Exit Reparse On Start
config_entries.description: Request the Nextion exit Active Reparse Mode before setup of the display. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: The lambda to use for rendering the content on the Nextion display. See [Rendering Lambda](https://esphome.io/components/display/nextion#display-nextion_lambda) for more information. This is typically empty. The individual components for the Nextion will handle almost all functions needed for updating display elements.

config_entries.label: Max Commands Per Loop
config_entries.description: Limits the number of commands processed per loop cycle. This helps prevent stack overflows when a large number of commands are queued. Lower values (for example, `20`  ) may help improve stability in constrained environments.

config_entries.label: Max Queue Age
config_entries.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for queued commands before they are automatically removed. This helps prevent stale commands from being executed after delays. Set to ``0`` to disable age-based removal (commands are only limited by queue size). **Note:** Very low values (e.g., \<50ms) may cause commands to be dropped before transmission due to ESPHome's loop timing (~16ms). Recommended minimum is 100ms when enabled. Range: 0-65535. Defaults to ``8000`` (8 seconds).

config_entries.label: Max Queue Size
config_entries.description: Sets the maximum number of commands that can be queued at once. When the limit is reached, new commands will be dropped and a warning will be logged. This helps prevent memory overflows or boot-time crashes in complex setups that issue a large number of commands in rapid succession. If not set, the queue size is unlimited.

config_entries.label: Skip Connection Handshake
config_entries.description: Sets whether the initial display connection handshake process is skipped. When set to `true`, the connection will be established without performing the handshake. This can be useful when using Nextion Simulator. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Start Up Page
config_entries.description: Sets the page to display when ESPHome connects to the Nextion. (Nextion shows page 0 on start-up by default).

config_entries.label: Startup Override Ms
config_entries.description: Time in milliseconds to wait before forcing the display to be marked as ready if it hasn't responded to the connection handshake. Set to ``0`` to disable the override and wait indefinitely for the handshake. This is useful for displays with slower startup sequences or to enforce strict handshake requirements. Range: 0-65535. Defaults to ``8000`` (8 seconds).

config_entries.label: TFT Upload HTTP Retries
config_entries.description: Number of retries for transient HTTP failures during TFT upload. On Arduino, this applies to the initial connection request. On ESP-IDF, this applies to chunk read operations. Range: 1-255. Defaults to `5`.

config_entries.label: TFT Upload HTTP Timeout
config_entries.description: HTTP request timeout for TFT upload transfers. Applies to each individual HTTP request during the upload process. Range: 1-65535ms. Defaults to `4.5s`.

config_entries.label: TFT Upload Watchdog Timeout
config_entries.description: Temporarily adjusts the system watchdog timeout for the duration of the TFT transfer process. The original timeout is automatically restored when the transfer completes (whether successful or not). May be useful on slow connections or when transferring large TFT files. Do not set this unless you are experiencing device reboots due to watchdog timeouts during TFT uploads; doing so may prevent the device from rebooting due to a legitimate problem. Only available on ESP32.

config_entries.label: TFT URL
config_entries.description: The URL from which to download the TFT file for display firmware updates (Nextion OTA). See [Nextion Upload](https://esphome.io/components/display/nextion#nextion_upload_tft).

config_entries.label: Touch Sleep Timeout
config_entries.description: Sets internal No-touch-then-sleep timer in seconds. Range: 0 (disabled) or 3-65535 seconds (max: ~18 hours). Values 1-2 are auto-corrected to 3. When set, Nextion will automatically enter sleep mode after the specified period of no touch activity. This setting persists until device reboot or reset. Note: The display will only wake up by restart or by configuring `auto_wake_on_touch: true`.

config_entries.label: Wake Up Page
config_entries.description: Sets the page to display after waking up.
